data.title: Analysts: MSTR buys in to ease pressure after Bitcoin whales sell off their holdings; market sentiment remains fragile.
data.description: Crypto analyst Murphy published an article on the X platform reviewing the market dynamics of the past week, revealing unusual Bitcoin fund flows: On March 11th and 13th, when BTC rebounded to $70,000–$71,000, a group of whale addresses (each holding over 1,000 BTC) sold 24,867 and 17,818 BTC respectively. The shares sold on March 11th had an average loss of 50% compared to cost, while the shares sold on March 13th had a loss of less than 10%. Analysis shows that these shares originated from purchases made at low prices in May–July 2025 and early April, with some exiting after significant losses and others being rotated out after precise bottom-fishing. Strategy bought another 22,337 BTC on March 17th, absorbing most of the excess supply. Nevertheless, this event reflects that market sentiment remains extremely fragile, and the patience of investors who have held BTC for nearly a year is gradually wearing thin. Future price rebounds may still trigger pressure from those holding losing positions.

data.title: Bitcoin and Ethereum ETFs Experience Consecutive Net Outflows
data.description: US spot Bitcoin ETFs recorded a net outflow of $52.1092 million on March 20, extending the streak to three consecutive days. According to NS3.AI, US spot Ethereum ETFs also experienced a third straight day of net outflows, amounting to $41.9715 million.

data.title: Greek Cargo Ship Enters Persian Gulf with AIS Active, Reports Say
data.description: A Greek bulk carrier, identified as the 'Giacometti,' has reportedly entered the Persian Gulf via the Strait of Hormuz with its Automatic Identification System (AIS) active, marking the first such occurrence since March 2. According to Jin10, the vessel, flying the Liberian flag, is owned by a Greek bulk shipping company. The AIS data indicates that the ship is transporting food cargo destined for Iran. On March 20, local time, the ship navigated around Larak Island, following a route similar to the 'safe corridor' delineated by Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps.

data.title: Bitdeer currently maintains zero holdings and sold 126.3 BTC this week.
data.description: Bitdeer, a Nasdaq-listed Bitcoin mining company, released its latest Bitcoin holdings data on the X platform. It still maintains zero Bitcoin holdings. As of the week ending March 20, its Bitcoin mining output was 126.3 BTC, but it sold 126.3 BTC during the same period.

data.title: Polymarket Teases Major Announcement Amid $20 Billion Valuation Speculation
data.description: A member of the Polymarket team has hinted at a significant announcement scheduled for next Monday. According to NS3.AI, this follows earlier reports suggesting that the platform was considering a financing round with a valuation of approximately $20 billion. Community members have speculated that the upcoming news could relate to either financing developments or a potential token launch.

data.title: Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $52.1092 million yesterday, marking the third consecutive day of net outflows.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $52.1092 million yesterday (March 20th, Eastern Time). The Bitcoin spot ETF with the largest single-day net inflow was the VanEck ETF HODL, with a net inflow of $2.9646 million. HODL's historical total net inflow has reached $1.182 billion. The Bitcoin spot ETF with the largest single-day net outflow was the BlackRock ETF IBIT, with a net outflow of $45.9441 million. IBIT's historical total net inflow has reached $63.257 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of Bitcoin spot ETFs is $90.301 billion, with an ETF net asset ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of Bitcoin's total market capitalization) of 6.44%. The historical cumulative net inflow has reached $56.231 billion.

data.title: Energy and Mineral Access in the Gulf Crucial for Technological Advancement
data.description: The potential of technology is closely tied to the availability of energy, metals, and minerals, which are currently restricted in the Gulf region. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the importance of these resources for technological progress. Access to these materials is essential for the development and implementation of new technologies, as they serve as fundamental components in various industries. The Gulf's current limitations on these resources pose significant challenges to technological advancement, emphasizing the need for strategic solutions to ensure their availability.

data.title: Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $41.9715 million yesterday, marking the third consecutive day of net outflows.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $41.9715 million yesterday (March 20th, Eastern Time). The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest single-day net inflow was the BlackRock Stalled ETH ETF (ETHB), with a net inflow of $5.4658 million. ETHB's historical total net inflow has reached $160 million. The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest single-day net outflow was the BlackRock ETF (ETHA), with a net outflow of $31.4508 million. ETHA's historical total net inflow has reached $11.912 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of Ethereum spot ETFs is $12.328 billion, with an ETF net asset value ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of total Ethereum market capitalization) of 4.79%, and a historical cumulative net inflow of $11.729 billion.

data.title: Controversy Surrounds Meme Coin Launch and Social Media Disappearance
data.description: Blockchain sleuth ZachXBT posted on X about a recent controversy involving a social media influencer who launched a meme coin despite warnings from the crypto community. Initially, the influencer began promoting meme coins, which led to widespread advice from the crypto community against launching a token. However, she proceeded with the launch, resulting in significant financial losses for her followers. Following the backlash, she blamed her partners for the situation and subsequently vanished from social media, leaving her followers without recourse. The incident has sparked discussions about accountability and the risks associated with meme coin investments.

data.title: Polymarket will announce major news next Monday.
data.description: Mustafa, a member of the Polymarket team, posted on the X platform that a major announcement is coming next Monday. Because the tweet included a coin symbol, the community speculates it may be related to a funding round or token launch. Previously, according to the Wall Street Journal, prediction market platform Kalshi and Polymarket were reportedly in talks with potential investors for new funding rounds, both targeting valuations of approximately $20 billion. Kalshi recently completed a new funding round of over $1 billion, reaching a valuation of $22 billion, more than double its $11 billion valuation in its previous funding round last December. Sources familiar with the matter revealed that this round was led by Coatue Management, and Kalshi currently has annualized revenue of $1.5 billion.

data.title: Bitcoin mining difficulty has dropped to 133.79 T, a decrease of 7.76%.
data.description: According to CloverPool data, Bitcoin mining difficulty was adjusted at block height 941,472 at 05:54 today, dropping to 133.79 T, a decrease of 7.76%, marking the second largest decrease since 2026. The current network hashrate is 933.51 EH/s. Analysis indicates that the next Bitcoin mining difficulty adjustment is expected to be further reduced, likely in 14 days, with a potential decrease of 0.4%.

data.title: Japan's Prime Minister Satuki Katayama: Strong Diplomacy to Maximize National Interests
data.description: Japan's Prime Minister Satuki Katayama has announced plans to actively pursue robust diplomatic efforts aimed at maximizing Japan's national interests. According to Jin10, she emphasized the importance of creating global peace and prosperity through these initiatives. Katayama's statement reflects Japan's commitment to enhancing its international presence and influence, focusing on strategic partnerships and collaborations to achieve these goals. Her approach underscores the significance of diplomacy in addressing global challenges and fostering a stable international environment.

data.title: Japan's Prime Minister Highlights Significance of U.S. Visit for Strengthening Alliance
data.description: Japan's Prime Minister, Satuki Katayama, emphasized the importance of her recent visit to the United States in confirming specific avenues for further strengthening the Japan-U.S. alliance and the economic development of both nations. According to Jin10, the visit holds profound significance in enhancing bilateral relations and exploring concrete measures for mutual growth. The discussions aimed at reinforcing the strategic partnership between the two countries, focusing on economic collaboration and shared interests.

data.title: Palantir AI system receives key project status from the U.S. Department of Defense.
data.description: The U.S. Department of Defense will designate Palantir Technologies' Maven artificial intelligence system as a "documented project." The Department is rapidly integrating the technology into its operational activities. Deputy Secretary of Defense Steve Feinberg announced this policy decision in a letter to other defense officials. Feinberg stated that designation as a "documented project" will facilitate the integrated application of the Maven system across the various branches of the military. Palantir's Maven intelligent system is a digital mission control platform that has been deployed in the Iran-Iraq War, assisting in the attack on 1,000 targets in the early stages of the conflict. (Reuters)

data.title: The UK has shut down cryptocurrency exchange Zedxion, accusing it of providing financial services to Iran.
data.description: British regulators have ordered the closure of cryptocurrency exchange Zedxion, accusing it of handling funds for Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC). An official statement said Zedxion was shut down because "information or statements in its company registration application were misleading, false, or deceptive." Media reports indicate that Zedxion handled approximately $1 billion for the IRGC, representing about 56% of the platform's total trading volume. This action by the UK follows measures taken by the U.S. Treasury Department against Zedxion for allegedly assisting Iran in circumventing international sanctions. Previously, the European Union also imposed sanctions on Zedxion for helping Iranian state-owned entities launder billions of dollars in oil revenue. Founded in 2021, Zedxion has Babak Morteza, an Iranian businessman accused of violating international sanctions, as a director and major controller. Governments worldwide are increasing scrutiny of cryptocurrency exchanges to prevent them from channeling funds to Iran, which is at war with the United States and Israel. (Yahoo)

data.title: U.S. Senate Fails to Advance Funding Bill for Homeland Security
data.description: The U.S. Senate has once again failed to advance a funding bill for the Department of Homeland Security, prolonging the department's shutdown that has lasted for over a month. According to Jin10, the funding for the department expired on February 13. This marks the fifth attempt by the Senate to move forward with the appropriations bill, which aims to fund the department until the end of the current fiscal year on September 30. The procedural vote on March 20 resulted in 47 votes in favor and 37 against, falling short of the 60 votes needed to advance the bill. Sixteen senators did not participate in the vote.

data.title: Plasma Token Unlock Scheduled for March 28
data.description: Plasma (XPL) is set to unlock approximately 88.89 million tokens on March 28 at 12:00 UTC+8. According to ChainCatcher, this token release is valued at around $8.51 million. The data comes from the Web3 asset data platform RootData, which tracks token unlock schedules.

data.title: Moonbirds to Unlock 27.08 Million Tokens on March 28
data.description: Moonbirds is set to unlock approximately 27.08 million tokens on March 28 at 13:00 UTC+8. According to ChainCatcher, this token release is part of the data provided by Web3 asset data platform RootData. The tokens are valued at around $4.22 million.

data.title: XRP Spot ETF Sees Significant Inflow Amid Market Activity
data.description: The U.S. XRP spot ETF experienced a notable single-day net inflow of $1.9782 million on March 20. According to NS3.AI, the Canary XRP ETF (XRPC) was the sole fund to report a net inflow on that day. The total net asset value of the XRP spot ETF reached $1.006 billion.

data.title: BTS Returns with Full Lineup for Seoul Performance Amid Heightened Security
data.description:
South Korean boy band BTS is making a comeback with a full lineup, performing today at Gwanghwamun Square in Seoul. According to RTHK, authorities estimate that between 170,000 to 260,000 people will gather in the vicinity. The government has issued a level two disaster crisis alert, 'Caution,' for Seoul's Jung-gu and Jongno-gu districts. Traffic control measures are in place around Gwanghwamun, with bus routes being diverted and several subway stations closed, preventing trains from stopping.

Yonhap News Agency reports that authorities have deployed 15,000 personnel to ensure safety. To manage the large crowd and potential terror threats, the police have stationed approximately 6,700 officers, including over 6,000 riot police. A significant number of female officers are tasked with security checks, and seven foreign affairs patrol teams have been mobilized to ensure the safety of international fans and address language needs. The event organizers have also arranged for 4,800 staff members to assist.

In the morning, police installed metal detectors at multiple entrances to rigorously check for dangerous items. Additionally, they are controlling access to 31 nearby office buildings to prevent spectators from gathering on rooftops. Anti-drone vehicles and portable jamming devices are being used to prevent illegal drone flights.
